summary: The canon-widened shared CanCastTo violates the JIT-EE compareTypesForCast contract: IsCanonicalCastTarget/IsCanonEquivalent make e.g. IFoo<__Canon>.CanCastTo(IFoo<string>) return TRUE ('castable for SOME instantiation'), but CorInfoImpl.compareTypesForCast passes positive results through as TypeCompareState.Must — its own comment table documents this case as May. The JIT/crossgen2 then folds/elides required runtime casts for shared generics, a type-safety miscompile (both ILC and R2R; the READYTORUN sanitizer only converts MustNot, not the new wrong Must).

System.Private.TypeLoader.csproj is given the REAL CastingHelper.Canon.cs (not the behavior-preserving NonCanon stub), silently changing the runtime type loader's CanCastTo semantics used by variant GVM dispatch resolution — a runtime behavior change with no test and no PR-visible justification (the 'include all .Canon.cs files' rule).

Every other consumer of the widened CanCastTo inherits the new optimistic semantics unaudited: DevirtualizationManager's guards, MetadataVirtualMethodAlgorithm's DIM/static-virtual variant resolution, MethodDesc covariant-return compatibility, TypeExtensions.MergeTypesToCommonParent/TryResolveConstraintMethodApprox, crossgen2's TypeValidationChecker/UnsafeAccessors, and ~10 downstream projects referencing ILCompiler.TypeSystem — none adjusted or tested by the PR.

MakeGenericMethodSite.InstantiateDependencies did NOT receive the NormalizeInstantiation() fix its structural twin MakeGenericTypeSite got, so MakeGenericMethod dataflow still checks constraints on (and roots) denormalized shapes — the same hazard class (#126604) on the method path, untested. (Some reviewers argue it is defensible via downstream GetCanonMethodTarget canonicalization.)

The new canonical branches of the real CanCastTo surface are essentially untested: IsCanonicalCastTarget's true-returning arms never execute in any test (deleting the hook leaves the suite green), no test exercises the broadened CanCastTo outside constraint checking, and there is zero coverage of compareTypesForCast/devirtualization/variance consumers with canonical inputs.

Doc/code mismatches in the new helpers: the 'pointers/byrefs/function pointers are not valid instantiation arguments' comment corresponds to no code check (the __UniversalCanon branch returns true unconditionally for them), IsCanonicalCastTarget's deliberate one-directional asymmetry is undocumented, and IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch's summary omits the structural/recursive half.

The __UniversalCanon<->__Canon cross-form matching (the '|| IsCanonicalDefinitionType(..., Any)' disjuncts) is unexplained and arguably over-permissive — __UniversalCanon also stands for value types that __Canon cannot represent.

Function-pointer canon shapes (e.g. delegate*<string,void> vs delegate*<__Canon,void>) hit no case in IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch and silently return false, reproducing #126604's bug class for function-pointer generics.

The Canon/NonCanon partial-file selection contract is entirely undocumented and only half-enforced: compile errors catch a missing/double include, but a WRONG choice is silent (a project picking NonCanon silently resurrects #126604; picking Canon silently widens semantics). C#9 extended partial methods (permitted at this LangVersion) would declare the seam; the stubs carry zero explanatory comments.

The canon-wildcard kernel rule is re-encoded in three/four places (CanCastToConstraintWithCanon duplicates IsCanonicalCastTarget verbatim; IsSpecialTypeMeetingConstraint re-derives it), inviting silent drift between copies.

IsCanonEquivalent's doc ('same type definition with all type arguments equal or canon-compatible') contradicts the code: the Length==0 early-return makes identical NON-generic pairs return false, an unexplained asymmetry.
IsCanonEquivalent's equal-arity invariant is implicit (no Debug.Assert unlike the sibling with identical logic), and the per-arg loop uses reference equality (==) instead of IsEquivalentTo.

Multiple new-code branches are untested: array rank/category mismatches (only rank-1 SzArrays covered), canon-on-the-left branches (C/D) of IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch, the mixed equal+canon instantiation path of IsCanonEquivalent (the actual #126604 shape), and no negative array test (int[] vs __Canon[]).

The IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch/IsCanonEquivalent mutual recursion carries no StackOverflowProtect/cycle guard, unlike every other recursive walk in the file (validators note the recursion is structurally decreasing, so this is a consistency/defensive concern, not a live bug).

The new helpers use TypeDesc.IsGCPointer, which is false for GenericParameterDesc even under a 'where T : class' constraint, unlike sibling code using HasReferenceTypeConstraint — an under-match concern (validators largely refuted a live impact since the checks are additive short-circuits).
IsSpecialTypeMeetingConstraint has no doc comment despite encoding the least-obvious policy of the five new helpers (why __Canon meets class/new() but not struct).

IsSpecialTypeMeetingConstraint's NotNullableValueTypeConstraint arm appears to be dead code — the caller's guard is already false for __UniversalCanon (the only type that could match), so the arm can never fire.

Naming drift in the new code: parameter 'type' vs sibling convention 'thisType'; 'IsCanonEquivalent' short-form vs sibling 'Canonical'; IsSpecialTypeMeetingConstraint breaks the file's Check* convention; NonVariantGenImpl breaks the ArgN fixture convention.

Unused `using System.Diagnostics;` left in TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers.NonCanon.cs — the Copilot bot flagged unused usings and the cleanup removed only one of the two (the Canon sibling genuinely needs it; the stub does not).

Project-file style inconsistencies: CastingHelper.Canon.cs entries spliced into the alphabetized Common block instead of the dedicated Canon block (two csproj files), and the new <Link> element uses 8-space indentation vs the file's 6-space convention.

The pre-existing value-type prefilter fires before any canon-awareness, so e.g. 'where T : U' with T=MyStruct<string>, U=MyStruct<__Canon> returns false even though the canon logic would match — canonical value-type generic shapes are shadowed.

Undocumented ordering dependencies: CanCastToConstraintWithCanon must run before the value-type short-circuit (because __UniversalCanon.IsValueType), and IsCanonicalCastTarget's early return must precede the category switch — neither load-bearing placement is commented.

Comment-contract mismatches in the constraints helper: CanCastToConstraintWithCanon's doc reads as a caller precondition but it is invoked unconditionally, and the 'any concrete type substituted at runtime will be validated then' claim is an absolute runtime guarantee not verifiable from this diff.

Variance/base-chain integration points are only shallowly tested: no covariant (out T) fixture at all, CanCastToClass's base-walk canon match only exercised at zero-hop depth, the self-interface IsCanonEquivalent check never covered, and no negative variance-path test.

Several new test assertions are vacuous/non-load-bearing: five Assert.True cases pass via pre-existing short-circuits (IsGCPointer etc.) before reaching the new canon code their comments credit, and two 'canonical variance' assertions pass via the ordinary variance path.

Factually wrong/self-contradictory test comments: a block calls IGen<in T> contravariant then labels the same slot 'invariant arg position'; 'U=int[] stays' contradicts array canonicalization; and the 'Parameterized canonical types (__Canon[] ...)' block header promises a scenario its body never constructs (duplicate of an earlier case).

The end-to-end smoke test is a weaker proxy for #126604: it exercises the wildcard-param branch rather than the structural interface match of the reported repro, has no negative case, and its only 'assertion' is .ToString() (did-not-throw).
The two new fixture types are fetched as method-locals inside the new [Fact], inconsistent with the class's 20+ constructor-initialized field fixtures.

Test-assembly fixture placement/naming quibbles: new types appended after the unrelated GenericMethods class rather than grouped with variant counterparts; NonVariantGenImpl diverges from the ArgN implementer naming.
RootingHelpers swallows all TypeSystemException from CheckCanGenerateMethod and returns false silently; when CheckConstraints fails the dependency list is silently empty with no diagnostic.
No test exercises the canonical constraint logic with a non-null InstantiationContext, unlike the real production caller.

Claim that System.Private.TypeLoader.csproj adds CastingHelper.Canon.cs but is missing the matching TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers Canon/NonCanon pair, unlike sibling projects. (Note: that project does not compile TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers.cs at all, so no pairing is required.)
The new smoke-test types use C# semicolon (empty-body) declarations, a style not used elsewhere in this particular file though established in sibling test files and fully valid at this LangVersion.
